prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: The lighthouse was his solemn refuge, a beacon of solitude amidst the tempestuous sea. John, the lighthouse keeper, left the hustle of the world below for this very reason: isolation. However, on this particular evening, as the thick, eerie fog engulfed the landscape, a muffled splash broke the usual lull. Intrigued, he ventured out. Cautiously, he crept along the craggy shore. The fog magnified every sound, every gull cry. Then he saw it: a dinghy, overturned and battered against the rocks. Carefully, he righted the craft, only to gasp in surprise. A sodden, shivering figure lay within: a young woman, her face concealed by a tangle of seaweed-entangled hair. Her ​​core temperature low, she was on the verge of succumbing to the icy embrace of the ocean. Resolute, John carried her back to the lighthouse, starting a fire in the hearth. Slowly, life returned to her limbs as the red light above them cast an otherworldly glow, shrouding both of them in a veil of uncertainty.
